footer_tagline: Open-source tools for creating, sharing, and visualizing energy forecasts.
footer_builtby: Built by AI Sweden. Open source under MIT license.
tool_demand_tagline: How much electricity will we need? Scenario-based demand projections from historical data, with interactive charts and geographic maps.
tool_generation_tagline: What do we need to build? PyPSA-based optimization to plan renewable capacity — solar, wind, storage — with weather-informed modeling.
framework_demand_description: A full-stack framework for forecasting electricity demand. Python generator, Node.js + DuckDB API, SvelteKit explorer. Fork it, swap in your data and scenarios, deploy.
framework_generation_description: A reusable framework for the Generation tool is not yet funded. PyPSA-VGR currently exists as a standalone implementation — contact us if you are interested in funding a framework extraction.
framework_empty_hint: No public framework repository yet. If you are interested in funding a reusable framework extraction, get in touch.
implementation_behovskartan_description: Sweden's electricity demand forecast explorer. Visualizes projected demand across regions, sectors, and scenarios through 2050. Built for Energimyndigheten.
implementation_pypsavgr_description: Renewable capacity planner for Västra Götaland. Uses PyPSA optimization to determine the solar, wind, and storage mix needed to meet future demand.
page_home_description: Open-source tools for creating, sharing, and visualizing energy forecasts.
page_tools_description: Two tools for energy forecasting: Demand and Generation. Both follow the same design philosophy — generator, API, frontend.
page_tools_demand_description: A full-stack framework for forecasting electricity demand. Python generator, Node.js + DuckDB API, SvelteKit explorer.
page_tools_generation_description: PyPSA-based optimization for planning renewable capacity — solar, wind, storage — with weather-informed modeling.
page_projects_description: Live applications built with the Energy Toolkit — across all tools.
page_getting_started_description: Fork a framework, point a coding agent at it, and customize it for your own region and data.
